title: House Passes Farm Bill with Bipartisan Vote Amid Food Security Concerns

contents:
# House Passes Farm Bill with Bipartisan Vote Amid Food Security Concerns

## Overview
The House of Representatives passed H.R. 7567, the Farm, Food, and National Security Act of 2026, with a 224-200 bipartisan vote, reauthorizing USDA programs through 2031 while facing criticism over inadequate SNAP funding.

## Details
The Farm Bill passed on April 30, 2026, reauthorizes SNAP and nutrition programs, enhances land conservation, and advances agricultural research through 2031. The legislation includes provisions to strengthen broadband access, the farm safety net, and specialty crop research. However, the Food Research & Action Center condemned the bill for failing to reverse $187 billion in SNAP cuts from prior budget reconciliation law H.R. 1. States will face increased administrative costs rising from 50% to 75% starting fiscal year 2027, with potential coverage of SNAP benefits beginning in 2028, creating concerns about eligibility cuts and hunger impacts.

## Location
Washington, D.C., United States

## Source Context
Reporting referenced from coverage tracked by the @TaFarms18 OSINT beat. The cited outlet provides additional context, attribution, and verification of the claims summarized above.

## Tags
farm bill, food security, SNAP, agriculture policy, nutrition programs, congressional vote, hunger crisis
